evals.prompt: I have a monorepo that uses both Claude Code and Codex. I want to add a hook that prevents the agent from writing to any file under packages/db/prisma/ because those are generated from upstream. Also block writes to .env files. Give me the complete configuration for both platforms and the shared script.
evals.expected_output: Should produce: 1) A shared bash script that blocks writes to prisma/ and .env paths, 2) Claude Code .claude/settings.json hooks config referencing the script, 3) Codex .codex/hooks.json config referencing the same script with statusMessage, 4) Both using PreToolUse with Write|Edit matcher

evals.prompt: My Claude Code hooks work great but I just added Codex to the project and my prompt-based PreToolUse hook that validates bash commands isn't doing anything on Codex. The hook config looks like this: {"type": "prompt", "prompt": "Check if this command is safe..."}. Why doesn't it work and how do I fix it for Codex while keeping the prompt hook on Claude Code?
evals.expected_output: Should explain: 1) Codex silently skips prompt hooks, 2) Need a command hook equivalent for Codex, 3) Provide a bash script that does deterministic validation, 4) Show both platform configs - Claude Code keeps prompt hook, Codex uses command hook, 5) Mention the degradation tradeoff (command hook won't catch as many edge cases)

evals.prompt: I want to set up hooks so the agent can't stop working until it has run the tests. This is for a TypeScript project using vitest. I need it to work on both Claude Code and Codex.
evals.expected_output: Should produce: 1) A Stop hook script that checks transcript for vitest/test execution, 2) Blocks stop if code was edited but no tests ran, 3) Both platform configurations, 4) Correct use of exit code 2 for blocking
